name: ◁ GRILLED MUSHROOMS WITH CRISPY GLUTINOUS PUFFS

ingredients.item:
GLUTINOUS PUFFS
3½ ounces (100 g) peeled split mung beans, rinsed, soaked, and drained
7 ounces (200 g) sticky rice, rinsed, soaked for 1 hour, and drained
ingredients.original:
GLUTINOUS PUFFS
3½ ounces (100 g) peeled split mung beans, rinsed, soaked, and drained
7 ounces (200 g) sticky rice, rinsed, soaked for 1 hour, and drained

ingredients.item: ¼ cup (50 g) sugar
ingredients.original: ¼ cup (50 g) sugar

ingredients.item: ½ cup (120 ml) full-fat, unsweetened coconut milk
ingredients.original: ½ cup (120 ml) full-fat, unsweetened coconut milk

ingredients.item: ¼ cup (50 g) water
ingredients.original: ¼ cup (50 g) water

ingredients.item: ¼ cup (60 ml) vegetable oil, plus more for deep-frying
ingredients.original: ¼ cup (60 ml) vegetable oil, plus more for deep-frying

ingredients.item:
FILLING
10½ ounces (300 g) king oyster mushrooms (see Note), rinsed with salted water
2 tablespoons vegetable oil, plus more for greasing
ingredients.original:
FILLING
10½ ounces (300 g) king oyster mushrooms (see Note), rinsed with salted water
2 tablespoons vegetable oil, plus more for greasing

ingredients.item: 2 cloves garlic, minced, or 1 tablespoon minced leek (white part only)
ingredients.original: 2 cloves garlic, minced, or 1 tablespoon minced leek (white part only)

ingredients.item: 1 tablespoon minced lemongrass
ingredients.original: 1 tablespoon minced lemongrass

ingredients.item: 2 tablespoons soy sauce
ingredients.original: 2 tablespoons soy sauce

ingredients.item: 1 tablespoon sugar
ingredients.original: 1 tablespoon sugar

ingredients.item: 1 teaspoon sriracha sauce
ingredients.original: 1 teaspoon sriracha sauce

ingredients.item: 1. MAKE THE GLUTINOUS PUFFS Combine the soaked mung beans and sticky rice in a colander and toss well.
ingredients.original: 1. MAKE THE GLUTINOUS PUFFS Combine the soaked mung beans and sticky rice in a colander and toss well.

ingredients.item: 2. In a small saucepan, heat ¼ cup (50 g) sugar and coconut milk over medium heat. Stir until sugar is completely dissolved, and then remove from heat.
ingredients.original: 2. In a small saucepan, heat ¼ cup (50 g) sugar and coconut milk over medium heat. Stir until sugar is completely dissolved, and then remove from heat.

ingredients.item: 3. Combine sticky rice, mung beans, coconut milk mixture, and water in a medium saucepan with a tight-fitting lid and stir. Bring to a boil and cook over medium heat until the water level drops below the surface of the beans and rice (about 5 minutes). Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for an additional 15 minutes without lifting the lid. Remove pot from heat and let sit for 5 minutes. Alternatively, you can combine all ingredients for the puffs in a rice cooker and press Start to cook.
ingredients.original: 3. Combine sticky rice, mung beans, coconut milk mixture, and water in a medium saucepan with a tight-fitting lid and stir. Bring to a boil and cook over medium heat until the water level drops below the surface of the beans and rice (about 5 minutes). Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for an additional 15 minutes without lifting the lid. Remove pot from heat and let sit for 5 minutes. Alternatively, you can combine all ingredients for the puffs in a rice cooker and press Start to cook.

ingredients.item: 4. While still hot, transfer the rice and beans to the greased bowl of a stand mixer. Attach the dough hook and knead on high speed, while gradually adding ¼ cup (60 ml) of vegetable oil. Knead until the rice and beans have broken down and a very smooth, thick, and sticky paste forms (about 10 minutes). Alternatively, transfer the rice and beans to a large, oiled metal bowl. Wearing rubber gloves, knead the mixture while it is still hot with your hands until a smooth, thick, and sticky paste forms (15 to 20 minutes).
ingredients.original: 4. While still hot, transfer the rice and beans to the greased bowl of a stand mixer. Attach the dough hook and knead on high speed, while gradually adding ¼ cup (60 ml) of vegetable oil. Knead until the rice and beans have broken down and a very smooth, thick, and sticky paste forms (about 10 minutes). Alternatively, transfer the rice and beans to a large, oiled metal bowl. Wearing rubber gloves, knead the mixture while it is still hot with your hands until a smooth, thick, and sticky paste forms (15 to 20 minutes).

ingredients.item: 5. Scrape down the sides of the bowl and transfer the paste to an oiled piece of plastic wrap large enough to enclose it. Using your hands, form the mixture into a cylinder about 2 inches (5 cm) in diameter and let cool.
ingredients.original: 5. Scrape down the sides of the bowl and transfer the paste to an oiled piece of plastic wrap large enough to enclose it. Using your hands, form the mixture into a cylinder about 2 inches (5 cm) in diameter and let cool.

ingredients.item: 6. Grease a knife with some vegetable oil, unwrap the rice and bean dough cylinder, and cut into ½-inch (1 cm) rounds. Roll out each round to reduce the thickness by half while keeping the shape.
ingredients.original: 6. Grease a knife with some vegetable oil, unwrap the rice and bean dough cylinder, and cut into ½-inch (1 cm) rounds. Roll out each round to reduce the thickness by half while keeping the shape.

ingredients.item: 7. Fill the bottom of a medium saucepan with 2 inches (5 cm) of oil and place over medium-low heat. Heat oil to 320°F (160°C). (You can check the temperature with a cooking thermometer or insert one end of a chopstick in the oil; if bubbles appear around it, the oil is ready for deep-frying). Dip a spatula into the oil to prevent sticking, and then gently drop in 1 or 2 sticky rice patties (any more and you risk them sticking). Fry over medium-low heat until they float to the surface and the exterior is no longer sticky (1 to 2 minutes). To make them puffy, press them into the pan with the spatula one or two times per side. When golden brown on one side, flip, and repeat on the other. Use a wire spider or slotted spoon to transfer each sticky rice puff to a cooling rack to drain.
ingredients.original: 7. Fill the bottom of a medium saucepan with 2 inches (5 cm) of oil and place over medium-low heat. Heat oil to 320°F (160°C). (You can check the temperature with a cooking thermometer or insert one end of a chopstick in the oil; if bubbles appear around it, the oil is ready for deep-frying). Dip a spatula into the oil to prevent sticking, and then gently drop in 1 or 2 sticky rice patties (any more and you risk them sticking). Fry over medium-low heat until they float to the surface and the exterior is no longer sticky (1 to 2 minutes). To make them puffy, press them into the pan with the spatula one or two times per side. When golden brown on one side, flip, and repeat on the other. Use a wire spider or slotted spoon to transfer each sticky rice puff to a cooling rack to drain.

ingredients.item: 8. MAKE THE FILLIING Cut the king oyster mushrooms lengthwise into slices about ¼-inch (6 mm) thick. Lightly score a ¼-inch (6 mm) diamond pattern on the slices, put them on a large rimmed plate, and set aside.
ingredients.original: 8. MAKE THE FILLIING Cut the king oyster mushrooms lengthwise into slices about ¼-inch (6 mm) thick. Lightly score a ¼-inch (6 mm) diamond pattern on the slices, put them on a large rimmed plate, and set aside.

ingredients.item: 9. In a small saucepan, heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil over medium-low heat. When hot, add garlic and lemongrass and cook and stir until slightly golden (about 1 minute). Reduce heat to low and add soy sauce and sugar. Simmer until sauce thickens (about 2 minutes), and then remove from heat and let cool.
ingredients.original: 9. In a small saucepan, heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil over medium-low heat. When hot, add garlic and lemongrass and cook and stir until slightly golden (about 1 minute). Reduce heat to low and add soy sauce and sugar. Simmer until sauce thickens (about 2 minutes), and then remove from heat and let cool.

ingredients.item: 10. With a rubber spatula, scrape all the marinade from the pan onto the mushroom plate. Use your fingers to coat the mushrooms thoroughly with the marinade and let them sit for 15 minutes.
ingredients.original: 10. With a rubber spatula, scrape all the marinade from the pan onto the mushroom plate. Use your fingers to coat the mushrooms thoroughly with the marinade and let them sit for 15 minutes.

ingredients.item: 11. After the 15 minutes, pour the excess marinade on the mushroom plate into a small saucepan and set aside.
ingredients.original: 11. After the 15 minutes, pour the excess marinade on the mushroom plate into a small saucepan and set aside.

ingredients.item: 12. Lightly coat an iron skillet or a pan with a heavy bottom with vegetable oil and place over high heat. When hot, add mushrooms and any marinade left on plate and cook until a noticeable brown crust forms (1 to 2 minutes per side). Use tongs to flip mushrooms.
ingredients.original: 12. Lightly coat an iron skillet or a pan with a heavy bottom with vegetable oil and place over high heat. When hot, add mushrooms and any marinade left on plate and cook until a noticeable brown crust forms (1 to 2 minutes per side). Use tongs to flip mushrooms.

ingredients.item: 13. Simmer the reserved marinade in the small saucepan over low heat until thickened (1 to 2 minutes), and then stir in sriracha.
ingredients.original: 13. Simmer the reserved marinade in the small saucepan over low heat until thickened (1 to 2 minutes), and then stir in sriracha.

ingredients.item: 14. To serve, assemble everything in the same order you would a burger: Place a glutinous puff on the bottom, followed by mushrooms, some cilantro, and a light drizzling of sauce. Top with a second glutinous puff, secure with a toothpick, and enjoy.
ingredients.original: 14. To serve, assemble everything in the same order you would a burger: Place a glutinous puff on the bottom, followed by mushrooms, some cilantro, and a light drizzling of sauce. Top with a second glutinous puff, secure with a toothpick, and enjoy.
